Title: Nucleotide Sugar Transporters: Orchestrating Luminal Glycosylation in Plants

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NST/TPT superfamily nucleotide sugar transporter; URGT family; predicted 10 transmembrane domains
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes UDP-Gal/UDP-Rha supply for leaf cell wall galactan biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. URGT1 transports UDP-Rha and UDP-Gal in vitro. 2. urgt1 mutants contain less cell wall Gal in leaves. 3. URGT1 overexpression increases pectic galactan, and promoter-swap experiments show functional equivalence with URGT2 but organ-specific expression dependence.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Proteoliposome transport assay; T-DNA mutant analysis; overexpression; promoter-swap complementation; monosaccharide/cell wall analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function appears organ specific; active in rosette leaves for wall Gal deposition
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall structure and polysaccharide assembly
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for glycoengineering to increase galactan content; stacking with UGE2 and GALS1 can strongly elevate wall Gal
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review summarizes that URGT1 and URGT2 are biochemically equivalent, with in planta differences likely due to expression pattern/dose dependence.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NST/TPT superfamily nucleotide sugar transporter; URGT family; predicted 10 transmembrane domains
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports seed mucilage biosynthesis; mutants have less mucilage and reduced Rha/GalA, indicating reduced RG-I
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. URGT2 transports UDP-Rha and UDP-Gal in vitro. 2. urgt2 mutants show altered seed mucilage and decreased Rha/GalA content. 3. URGT2 functions redundantly with URGT4/URGT6 and is interchangeable with URGT1 when expressed from the appropriate promoter.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Proteoliposome transport assay; T-DNA mutant analysis; higher-order mutants; promoter-swap complementation; ruthenium red staining; mucilage sugar analysis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate seed coat mucilage and pectin traits
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TWAS in cotton identified URGT2 ortholog associated with fiber cell development; coexpressed with pectin-related transport systems in some contexts.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NST/TPT superfamily nucleotide sugar transporter; URGT family
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Biochemically transports UDP-Rha and UDP-Gal; specific in planta phenotype not established in detail in the review
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. URGT3 is a bifunctional UDP-Rha/UDP-Gal transporter. 2. Included among six Arabidopsis URGT transporters identified by competitive proteoliposome assays. 3. In planta role remains less defined than URGT1/2/4/6.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Proteoliposome transport assay
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for future glycoengineering of pectin-associated sugar flux
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as under circadian regulation in the review.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NST/TPT superfamily nucleotide sugar transporter; URGT family
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to mucilage RG-I biosynthesis and elongation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. URGT4 transports UDP-Rha and UDP-Gal. 2. Higher-order mutant analyses identified URGT4, together with URGT2 and URGT6, as having discrete functions in seed mucilage RG-I biosynthesis. 3. URGT2 and URGT4 are mainly involved in RG-I chain elongation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Proteoliposome transport assay; mutant and higher-order mutant analysis; mucilage composition analysis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for modifying seed mucilage properties
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also referred to as AtNST-KT4 in the review.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NST/TPT superfamily nucleotide sugar transporter; URGT family
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Biochemically transports UDP-Rha and UDP-Gal; direct in planta phenotype not defined here
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. URGT5 is a UDP-Rha/UDP-Gal transporter. 2. The review notes widespread redundancy among URGT family members. 3. Transcriptomic evidence indicates expression during gametogenesis in some datasets.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Proteoliposome transport assay; transcriptomic profiling
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named as URT5/UTr5 in some transcriptomic context in the review, but core characterized transporter family designation is URGT5.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NST/TPT superfamily nucleotide sugar transporter; URGT family
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Affects adherent mucilage biosynthesis and contributes to seed mucilage RG-I formation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. URGT6 transports UDP-Rha and UDP-Gal. 2. Higher-order mutant analysis implicated URGT6 in mucilage biosynthesis. 3. Unlike URGT2/4, urgt6 primarily affected adherent mucilage biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Proteoliposome transport assay; higher-order mutant analysis; mucilage profiling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for altering adherent mucilage properties

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NST/TPT superfamily UDP-Xyl transporter; predicted 10 transmembrane domains
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for proper xylan biosynthesis; uxt1 reduces stem Xyl and GlcA, with stronger effects in young stems
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UXT1 transports UDP-Xyl and weakly UDP-Arap. 2. uxt1 mutants show reduced Xyl and GlcA in stems. 3. Combined loss with UXT2/UXT3 causes delayed growth, darker plants, shorter stems, collapsed vessels, and defective pollination.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Proteoliposome transport assay; T-DNA single/double/triple mutants; monosaccharide analysis; anatomical analysis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Secondary wall formation and reproductive development
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to lower xylan for biomass processing, though higher-order loss causes developmental defects
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Expression reported to be regulated by long intergenic noncoding RNAs
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GWAS in Populus associated UXT1 ortholog with ecological traits; grape UXT1 is upregulated by gibberellin.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions redundantly with UXT1/UXT3 in xylan biosynthesis; single effect limited but contributes to higher-order mutant phenotypes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UXT2 transports UDP-Xyl with minor UDP-Arap activity. 2. Single mutants have little visible phenotype. 3. Combined mutation with UXT1/UXT3 exacerbates xylan deficiency and growth defects.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Proteoliposome transport assay; higher-order mutant analysis

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Redundant contributor to xylan biosynthesis; participates in severe triple-mutant wall and growth phenotypes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UXT3 transports UDP-Xyl with minor UDP-Arap activity. 2. Single effects are limited. 3. In combination with uxt1 and uxt2, loss causes strong developmental and secondary wall defects.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Proteoliposome transport assay; higher-order mutant analysis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: UDP-Glc-dependent ER glycoprotein reglucosylation; also reported UDP-Gal transport in early assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports ER quality control and gametophyte development; double mutant with utr3 is lethal
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UTr1 complements UDP-Gal transport-deficient cells and is linked to UDP-Glc/UDP-Gal transport activity. 2. UTr1 is ER localized and induced by tunicamycin/DTT. 3. utr1 utr3 double mutants are lethal and show pollen/embryo sac transmission defects, indicating a role in ER quality control and reproduction.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast/cell complementation; localization; stress-responsive expression analysis; mutant genetics
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not an easy engineering target because combined loss with UTr3 is lethal

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Biochemically characterized as UDP-Gal transporter; in planta phenotype not defined
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UTr2 transports UDP-Gal. 2. Transcriptomic data suggest upregulation in root cap development. 3. Direct mutant phenotype remains undetermined in the review.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transport assay; transcriptomic analysis

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports ER stress response and reproductive development; loss together with UTr1 is lethal
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UTr3 transports UDP-Glc in proteoliposomes and is distributed in ER and Golgi. 2. UTr3 is induced by tunicamycin and DTT, linking it to unfolded protein response. 3. Double mutation with UTr1 disrupts pollen and embryo sac development and is lethal.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Proteoliposome transport assay; localization; stress-induced expression; mutant genetics
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ER quality control and glycoprotein folding

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss causes accelerated lateral root growth and abnormal root hairs under high sucrose
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UTr7 transports UDP-Gal and UDP-Glc in vitro. 2. Loss-of-function mutants show accelerated lateral root growth and abnormal root hairs in high sucrose. 3. No major change in total seedling monosaccharide composition was observed, suggesting tissue-specific roles.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transport assay; mutant phenotyping; immunolabeling
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transcriptomic studies indicate increased expression during gametogenesis.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Pectin-rich seed mucilage; UDP-GlcA/UDP-GalA-dependent wall polymers
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seed mucilage; root and pollen tube walls
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for proper seed mucilage composition; uuat1 lowers soluble mucilage GalA/Rha/Xyl and affects extracellular matrix properties
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UUAT1 transports UDP-GlcA and UDP-GalA in vitro. 2. uuat1 mutants alter seed mucilage composition and distal cell wall arabinan, with lower Ara in roots and pollen tubes. 3. Loss causes pleiotropic extracellular matrix effects, including increased homogalacturonan methylation in seed mucilage.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant screen; transport assay; monosaccharide analysis; mucilage characterization
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pectin biosynthesis and extracellular matrix organization
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for modifying pectin-rich mucilage and extracellular matrix traits
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Coexpressed with GoSAMT1/2 in pectin methylesterification-related networks.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specific UDP-Araf transporter; single mutant has no obvious phenotype under standard conditions
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UAfT1 specifically transports UDP-Araf. 2. Single mutants are morphologically similar to wild type, indicating redundancy. 3. UAfT family overexpression supports increased Ara accumulation in walls.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Proteoliposome transport assay; mutant analysis; overexpression studies

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression can raise Ara content; single mutant shows little phenotype
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UAfT2 specifically transports UDP-Araf. 2. Single mutants are largely normal, indicating redundancy. 3. Overexpression under CaMV 35S can increase leaf wall Ara and occasionally cause developmental defects.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Proteoliposome transport assay; overexpression; monosaccharide analysis
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Regulated by long noncoding RNAs according to the review

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases Ara and can cause stunted growth and early leaf yellowing
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UAfT3 specifically transports UDP-Araf. 2. Single mutant phenotype is minor, but overexpression strongly alters Ara content and growth. 3. Glycome profiling showed altered pectin and AGP epitopes in overexpressors.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Proteoliposome transport assay; overexpression; glycome profiling; mutant analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression can cause stunted growth and early yellowing
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Regulated by miRNA and ubiquitylation-mediated degradation according to the review

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Leaf walls of uaf4 mutants contain significantly less Ara; overexpression increases Ara and causes strong developmental defects
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UAfT4 specifically transports UDP-Araf. 2. uaf4 leaf walls show reduced Ara, supporting a contribution to wall arabinosylation. 3. Overexpression strongly increases Ara and causes stunting/early yellowing.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Proteoliposome transport assay; mutant analysis; overexpression; monosaccharide analysis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Golgi-derived glycans in leaves and seeds
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major Golgi UDP-GlcNAc transporter required for GlcNAc addition to complex N-glycans and GIPCs
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGNT1 specifically transports UDP-GlcNAc efficiently under competitive conditions. 2. ugnt1 mutants alter N-glycan profiles, causing depletion of complex/hybrid N-glycans and enrichment of high-Man structures. 3. GlcNAc-containing GIPC biosynthesis also depends on UGNT1.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Proteoliposome transport assay; mutant glycomics; sphingolipid profiling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target for glycoengineering of plant glycoproteins and sphingolipids

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: UDP-GlcNAc/possibly related ER glycosylation substrate; cytokinin-related developmental outputs
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Controls shoot apical meristem activity and cytokinin responses; transport role differs from UGNT1 and may not be physiological UDP-GlcNAc transport
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ROCK1 was identified as an ER-localized UDP-GlcNAc/UDP-GalNAc transporter linked to cytokinin responses. 2. rock1 mutants have enlarged, more active inflorescence meristems and elevated cytokinin responses. 3. Unlike ugnt1, rock1 mutants do not alter N-glycan or GIPC profiles, suggesting ROCK1 may transport another structurally related substrate in vivo.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transport assays; mutant phenotyping; glycan/sphingolipid profiling
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Meristem regulation and ER-associated metabolic transport
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression is under circadian regulation; transcriptomics indicate importance for pollen exine formation.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong developmental alteration of meristem function when mutated

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides GDP-Man for GIPC glycosylation; mutants are dwarfed with defense activation and impaired seed set
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GONST1 complements yeast GDP-Man transport mutants and transports GDP-Man in vitro. 2. gonst1 mutants show severe growth and defense phenotypes. 3. The primary in planta defect is in Man-containing GIPCs rather than expected wall glucomannan.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast complementation; transport assay; T-DNA mutant analysis; sphingolipid analysis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sphingolipid glycosylation important for growth and defense
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation causes dwarfism, poor growth on soil, impaired seed set, and constitutive defense
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although in vitro capable of transporting multiple GDP-sugars, in planta specificity appears centered on GIPC glycosylation.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functionally overlaps with GONST1 in GIPC glycosylation; single mutant is mild, but double mutant enhances defects
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GONST2 transports GDP-Man and weakly GDP-Gal. 2. gonst2 single mutants resemble wild type, but gonst1 gonst2 double mutants are more severe than gonst1. 3. GONST2 contributes to pathogen resistance phenotypes and can rescue gonst1 when expressed from the GONST1 promoter.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transport assay; mutant and double-mutant analysis; promoter-swap rescue
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Biotrophic pathogen Golovinomyces orontii; necrotrophic pathogen Botrytis cinerea
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: gonst2 mutants show increased resistance to Golovinomyces orontii but not Botrytis cinerea.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential GDP-Fuc transporter required for plant fucosylation and normal development; silencing drastically reduces Fuc and causes severe defects
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GFT1 specifically transports GDP-Fuc in exchange for GMP. 2. Strong silencing causes lethality and survivors have very low cell wall and protein Fuc. 3. Fuc transport is indispensable for plant development.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Proteoliposome transport assay; RNAi silencing; wall and protein sugar analysis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fucosylation of wall polymers and glycoproteins needed for development
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing is often lethal and surviving plants are severely dwarfed/dark
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously named GONST4 in the review.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for GDP-Gal supply to RG-II; silencing reduces L-Gal in RG-II, decreases RG-II dimer stability, and causes dwarfism reversible by boric acid
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Genetic evidence supports GGLT1 as the GDP-Gal transporter, despite lack of standard biochemical assay due to expression limitations. 2. Silencing reduces L-Gal in RG-II by up to 50% and compromises RG-II cross-linking. 3. Plants are dwarfed and salt sensitive, but growth can be rescued by elevated boric acid.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi silencing; RG-II composition analysis; boric acid rescue; stress phenotyping
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: RG-II integrity and cell wall stability
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing causes dwarfism and salt-sensitive compromised cell wall integrity
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review notes naming ambiguity with previous GONST nomenclature and states genetic analyses clearly indicate GDP-Gal transporter function.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Implicated in UDP-Glc transport into the rice Golgi
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNST1 was identified as involved in UDP-Glc transport into the Golgi apparatus of rice. 2. The exact Golgi glycoconjugates depending on this activity remain unclear according to the review.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene characterization summarized in review
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The specific downstream glycoconjugate targets are unresolved.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NST/TPT superfamily putative CMP-Kdo transporter/CMP-sialic acid transporter-like protein
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate transporter for CMP-Kdo, but function remains unconfirmed in plants
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. At5g41760 transports CMP-sialic acid in heterologous assays, though sialic acid is absent from plants. 2. Because CMP-Kdo is needed for RG-II biosynthesis, the gene is proposed as a putative CMP-Kdo transporter. 3. Plant-essentiality and biochemical confirmation for CMP-Kdo transport remain unresolved.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous complementation/transport assay
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review treats this as a putative rather than confirmed plant CMP-Kdo transporter.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NST/TPT superfamily putative CMP-Kdo transporter/CMP-sialic acid transporter-like protein
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed essential transporter possibly involved in CMP-Kdo transport for RG-II biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. At4g35335 is the Arabidopsis ortholog of rice OSCSTLP2 discussed as a putative CMP-Kdo transporter. 2. Homozygous mutants could not be obtained, suggesting essentiality. 3. Direct biochemical demonstration of CMP-Kdo transport is still lacking.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative orthology; mutant recovery attempts
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Expression regulated by recursive splicing according to the review
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function remains hypothetical and requires direct biochemical confirmation.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NST/TPT superfamily CMP-sialic acid transporter-like protein
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transports CMP-sialic acid in vitro and is proposed as a possible CMP-Kdo transporter candidate in rice
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OSCSTLP1 transports CMP-sialic acid in vitro. 2. Because plants lack sialic acid, the protein is proposed to possibly transport CMP-Kdo for RG-II biosynthesis. 3. This remains unconfirmed.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous transport assay

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NST/TPT superfamily CMP-sialic acid transporter-like protein
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Closest homolog of OSCSTLP1 but did not transport CMP-sialic acid in vitro; remains a putative CMP-Kdo-related transporter candidate
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OSCSTLP2 is homologous to OSCSTLP1 and orthologous to Arabidopsis At4g35335. 2. It did not transport CMP-sialic acid in vitro. 3. Potential CMP-Kdo transport role is unresolved.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous transport assay
